edges.description: Activation of testis-determination programs in 46,XX gonadal tissue produces testicular but typically dysgenetic gonads with abnormal seminiferous tubule architecture and absent germ cells.
edges.source: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

edges.description: Ectopic SRY translocated onto an X chromosome (or rarely an autosome) drives the testis-determination cascade in the otherwise 46,XX gonad, producing the inappropriate activation of male sex determination that is the proximal disease mechanism in SRY-positive cases.
edges.source: SRY translocation onto an X chromosome
edges.target: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

edges.description: Gain-of-function dysregulation of pro-testis genes (SOX9 duplication or its RevSex enhancer, ectopic SOX3) substitutes for SRY and triggers the testis-determination cascade in 46,XX gonadal precursors, producing the inappropriate male sex-determination program that defines the SRY-negative subtype.
edges.source: SOX9 or SOX3 dysregulation in SRY-negative cases
edges.target: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

edges.description: Loss of RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling removes a female-gonad-determining input, allowing testis differentiation in the 46,XX gonad.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced canonical beta-catenin signaling impairs female gonadal differentiation.
edges.source: RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling loss
edges.target: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

edges.description: Biallelic RSPO1 loss produces the clinically distinctive SRY-negative 46,XX DSD subtype with palmoplantar hyperkeratosis/keratoderma.
intermediate_mechanisms: altered RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin signaling in skin
edges.source: RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling loss

edges.description: The RSPO1-associated palmoplantar hyperkeratosis/sex-reversal syndrome includes risk of cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma.
intermediate_mechanisms: altered RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin signaling in skin
edges.source: RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling loss
edges.target: Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Skin Risk

edges.description: Reduced NR5A1-dependent female/anti-testis restraint permits the male developmental pathway to dominate in 46,XX gonadal tissue, feeding into the shared inappropriate testis-determination program.
intermediate_mechanisms: Downregulation of female antitestis genes reduces inhibition of the male developmental pathway.
edges.target: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

edges.description: Dysgenetic testes produce inadequate testosterone and inhibin, leading to compensatory pituitary gonadotropin elevation and a relative imbalance that promotes gynecomastia at puberty.
Dysgenetic 46,XX testes lack the Y-chromosomal AZF regions required for spermatogenesis, producing azoospermia.
Dysgenetic testicular development produces small testes, the most common clinical finding in the adult presentation.
Azoospermia from dysgenetic testes and absent AZF regions causes the adult infertility presentation.
Incompletely masculinized and dysgenetic testicular development can include undescended testes, especially in SRY-negative presentations.
intermediate_mechanisms: incomplete fetal androgen and testicular descent signaling

edges.description: Incomplete androgenization of the developing external genitalia can produce hypospadias in 46,XX testicular DSD, particularly SRY-negative cases.
intermediate_mechanisms: incomplete fetal androgen signaling during urethral development

edges.description: Testicular failure lowers sex-steroid feedback and elevates gonadotropins, producing hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.
Testicular failure and relative estrogen-androgen imbalance can produce gynecomastia.
Inadequate androgen action in the developing or pubertal phenotype can produce small penis size.

edges.target: SRY translocation onto an X chromosome
SOX9 or SOX3 dysregulation in SRY-negative cases
SOX9 or SOX3 dysregulation in SRY-negative cases
RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling loss

nodes.description: Affected individuals are uniformly azoospermic because the 46,XX karyotype lacks the AZF spermatogenesis loci required for sperm production.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: All patients were azoospermic, due to the lack of AZF genetic regions.

nodes.description: Cryptorchidism is more common in SRY-negative cases and reflects incomplete androgen-driven testicular descent during fetal development.

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Cryptorchidism is present in 15% and anterior hypospadias in around 10%.

nodes.description: Small testes are the most common physical finding, reflecting the dysgenetic testicular tissue and impaired spermatogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Small testes were the most common clinical characteristic present in 90.2% of the patients

nodes.description: Although ectopic testis-determination signals successfully divert the 46,XX gonad toward testicular development, the absence of the full Y chromosome (and AZF spermatogenesis loci) leaves the resulting testis architecturally abnormal, with progressive loss of germ cells and Sertoli-cell-only seminiferous tubules in the adult.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: All patients were azoospermic, due to the lack of AZF genetic regions.

nodes.description: Pubertal breast development occurs in a subset of patients, attributed to a relatively elevated estrogen:androgen ratio in the setting of testicular failure.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Small testes were the most common clinical characteristic present in 90.2% of the patients, followed by small penis (31.8%), gynecomastia (26.8%) and poor hair distribution (15.4%).

nodes.description: Reduced testosterone production and elevated FSH and LH define the endocrine pattern, reflecting failure of the dysgenetic testes to maintain normal steroidogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: All patients included in the review presented hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Compromised testicular steroidogenesis lowers serum testosterone, removes feedback inhibition on the hypothalamic-pituitary axis, and elevates LH and FSH. The relative estrogen excess at puberty contributes to gynecomastia, while inadequate androgen drives small testes, sparse body hair, and reduced fertility.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: All patients included in the review presented hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Hypospadias is more frequent in SRY-negative cases due to incomplete androgenization of the developing genital tubercle.

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Cryptorchidism is present in 15% and anterior hypospadias in around 10%.

nodes.description: The shared proximal disease mechanism is activation of the male testis-determination cascade in a chromosomally female (46,XX) gonad, diverting bipotential gonadal precursors toward testicular rather than ovarian differentiation. In SRY-positive cases this is driven by ectopic SRY transferred onto an X chromosome or autosome; in SRY-negative cases pro-testis genes (SOX9, SOX3) are inappropriately overexpressed or pro-ovarian genes (RSPO1) lose function.
nodes.id: Inappropriate activation of testis determination in 46,XX gonad

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The SRY gene is identified as the main gene regulating the testes determination cascade. The most important role of SRY is to regulate the SOX9 expression in Sertoli cell precursors. This pathway, in turn, activates testis-specific genes leading to testis determination

evidence.reference_title: Duplication of SOX3 in an SRY-negative 46,XX male with prostatic utricle: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: SRY-negative 46,XX males show overexpression of pro-testis genes, such as SOX9 and SOX3, or failure of pro-ovarian genes, such as WNT4 and RSPO1, which induces testis differentiation

nodes.description: Affected individuals are infertile because of azoospermia from the dysgenetic testes; testicular sperm extraction is generally not successful.

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Male adults with 46,XX and normal external genitalia generally discover their pathology in adulthood because of infertility.

nodes.description: Small penis size is a recognized clinical feature of 46,XX testicular DSD, reflecting incomplete androgen-driven phallic development; cohort data report it in approximately one-third of affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: small penis (31.8%)

evidence.reference_title: NR5A1 is a novel disease gene for 46,XX testicular and ovotesticular disorders of sex development.
evidence.snippet: We propose NR5A1, previously associated with 46,XY DSD and 46,XX primary ovarian insufficiency, as a novel gene for 46,XX (ovo)testicular DSD.

evidence.reference_title: A recurrent p.Arg92Trp variant in steroidogenic factor-1 (NR5A1) can act as a molecular switch in human sex development.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that a specific recurrent heterozygous missense mutation (p.Arg92Trp) in the accessory DNA-binding region of NR5A1 is associated with variable degree of testis development in 46,XX children and adults from four unrelated families.

evidence.reference_title: Nuclear Receptor Gene Variants Underlying Disorders/Differences of Sex Development through Abnormal Testicular Development.
evidence.snippet: NR5A1 variants are associated with 46,XY DSD and 46,XX testicular/ovotesticular DSD.

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: we suggest searching for mutations of other genes involved in the sex determination cascade such as SOX9, SOX3, DAX1, WT1, FGF9, and SF1.

nodes.description: Recurrent heterozygous NR5A1 p.Arg92Trp variants can shift 46,XX fetal gonadal fate away from stable ovarian differentiation and toward SRY-independent testicular differentiation, probably by reducing female/anti-testis restraint of the male developmental pathway.

evidence.reference_title: NR5A1 is a novel disease gene for 46,XX testicular and ovotesticular disorders of sex development.
evidence.snippet: We hypothesize that p.(Arg92Trp) results in decreased inhibition of the male developmental pathway through downregulation of female antitestis genes, thereby tipping the balance toward testicular differentiation in 46,XX individuals.

evidence.reference_title: A recurrent p.Arg92Trp variant in steroidogenic factor-1 (NR5A1) can act as a molecular switch in human sex development.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that a specific recurrent heterozygous missense mutation (p.Arg92Trp) in the accessory DNA-binding region of NR5A1 is associated with variable degree of testis development in 46,XX children and adults from four unrelated families.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function mutations in RSPO1 cause palmoplantar hyperkeratosis as a pathognomonic extra-gonadal feature of the RSPO1-associated form of SRY-negative 46,XX testicular DSD. Skin involvement is the cardinal differentiating finding from other SRY-negative causes and is diagnostically informative for the RSPO1 subtype.

evidence.reference_title: Novel RSPO1 mutation causing 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development with palmoplantar keratoderma: A review of literature and expansion of clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Palmoplantar hyperkeratosis with squamous cell carcinoma of skin and sex reversal (MIM # 610644) is a clinically distinctive form of SRY-negative 46,XX disorder of sex development.

evidence.reference_title: Novel RSPO1 mutation causing 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development with palmoplantar keratoderma: A review of literature and expansion of clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: It is a rare autosomal recessive disorder caused due to biallelic loss of function mutations in RSPO1 gene.

nodes.description: In the rare autosomal recessive RSPO1-associated subset of SRY-negative 46,XX testicular DSD, biallelic RSPO1 loss of function reduces canonical beta-catenin signaling that normally promotes female gonadal differentiation. Loss of this pro-ovarian signal permits the 46,XX gonad to enter the testis-determination program.
nodes.id: RSPO1-dependent canonical beta-catenin pro-ovarian signaling loss

evidence.reference_title: Novel RSPO1 mutation causing 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development with palmoplantar keratoderma: A review of literature and expansion of clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: RSPO1 acts by activating the canonical β-catenin pathway and is one of the most important genes controlling female gonadal differentiation.

evidence.reference_title: Duplication of SOX3 in an SRY-negative 46,XX male with prostatic utricle: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: We report the first case of an SRY-negative 46 XX male with prostatic utricle caused by SOX3 duplication.

evidence.reference_title: A Korean boy with 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development caused by SOX9 duplication.
evidence.snippet: SOX9 duplication has been found to be a rare cause of 46,XX testicular DSD in humans.

evidence.reference_title: Whole genome sequencing identifies a cryptic SOX9 regulatory element duplication underlying a case of 46,XX ovotesticular difference of sexual development.
evidence.snippet: whole genome sequencing reported a pathogenic duplication in a non-coding region that contains the RevSex regulatory element, which modifies SOX9 expression and is associated with 46,XX OT-DSD and complete sex reversal

nodes.description: In SRY-negative 46,XX testicular DSD, gain-of-function lesions in pro-testis genes substitute for SRY. Duplications of SOX9 (or its RevSex upstream regulatory element) and ectopic activation of SOX3 (an X-linked SRY paralog) drive testis determination in the absence of SRY.
nodes.id: SOX9 or SOX3 dysregulation in SRY-negative cases

evidence.reference_title: A Korean boy with 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development caused by SOX9 duplication.
evidence.snippet: overexpression of SOX9 leads to the male development of 46,XX gonads in the absence of SRY

evidence.reference_title: Whole genome sequencing identifies a cryptic SOX9 regulatory element duplication underlying a case of 46,XX ovotesticular difference of sexual development.
evidence.snippet: whole genome sequencing reported a pathogenic duplication in a non-coding region that contains the RevSex regulatory element, which modifies SOX9 expression and is associated with 46,XX OT-DSD and complete sex reversal

evidence.reference_title: Duplication of SOX3 in an SRY-negative 46,XX male with prostatic utricle: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: SOX3 duplication may cause sex reversal, and all 46,XX SRY-negative males should be screened for SOX3 mutations.

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Genetic analyses showed translocation of the SRY on Xp chromosome and complete absence of all Azoospermia factor (AZF) genetic regions.

nodes.description: The pathognomonic genetic lesion in SRY-positive 46,XX testicular DSD is a Y-to-X translocation during paternal meiosis, producing an X chromosome that carries SRY. The translocated SRY drives the testis determination cascade despite the otherwise female chromosomal complement.
nodes.id: SRY translocation onto an X chromosome

evidence.reference_title: 46,XX Testicular Disorder of Sex Development (DSD): A Case Report and Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Various studies indicated that 80–90% of 46,XX males result from a Y to X translocation during meiosis

evidence.reference_title: A 46,XX Karyotype in Men with Infertility: Two New Cases and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: The presence of the SRY was identified in 130/154 (84.4%) patients: in 98.5% of cases, it was translocated on the Xp chromosome and in 1.5% on an autosome.

nodes.description: RSPO1-associated SRY-negative 46,XX testicular DSD is described as a palmoplantar hyperkeratosis syndrome with squamous cell carcinoma of skin, making cutaneous carcinoma risk a clinically significant feature of this molecular subtype.
nodes.id: Squamous Cell Carcinoma of Skin Risk

evidence.reference_title: Novel RSPO1 mutation causing 46,XX testicular disorder of sex development with palmoplantar keratoderma: A review of literature and expansion of clinical phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Palmoplantar hyperkeratosis with squamous cell carcinoma of skin and sex reversal (MIM # 610644) is a clinically distinctive form of SRY-negative 46,XX disorder of sex development.
